page: Vultr vc2-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual machine in the Cloud Compute family, configured with 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This single-core, single-threaded instance is paired with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes 55 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU features 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and supports hardware instructions such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography.

Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers, reflecting its shared-core allocation. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both scored 1211.89. Memory bandwidth is limited, measuring 7.23 GB/sec for large-block reads and 5.37 GB/sec for writes, though cached small-block reads reach 85.9 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value transactions are modest, with Redis SET operations executing at 0.67 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 285.19.

Given its shared hardware resources and entry-level performance profile, this instance offers a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads where continuous high CPU utilization is not required. It is equipped with one complimentary public IPv4 address. The server is best suited for low-traffic static web serving, basic utility scripting, lightweight development environments, or small-scale testing pipelines.
description: Vultr vc2-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual machine from the Cloud Compute family, featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 55 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this single-core instance is designed for budget-conscious, low-intensity applications. Benchmark metrics indicate modest performance, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1211.89 and Redis SET throughput of 0.67 million operations per second. Large-block memory read bandwidth sits at 7.23 GB/sec. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for workloads that do not demand sustained compute power. It is best suited for running lightweight utility scripts, hosting low-traffic static websites, or serving as a basic development and testing environment.
